focus: [D3-A10/D3] standing routing self-test gold corpus for prompt->expected workflowMode, hint-free plus adversarial minimal pairs

graphEvents.label: Can sk-design routing be measured by a private-gold prompt-to-workflowMode corpus before utilization proof is scored?
sk-design has stable workflowMode labels, but the deterministic benchmark scores intent/resource primitives rather than a hard hub-route primitive
Lane C already has the right public/private and contamination-lint mechanism, but private expected gold lacks workflowMode and minimal-pair fields
Existing modePrecision is explicitly advisory and cannot enforce parent-hub workflowMode selection
sk-design route regressions are near-neighbor confusions, so the corpus needs adversarial minimal pairs rather than only one happy-path fixture per mode
For parent-mode skills, expected workflowMode or routeOutcome must pass before resource recall, context manifests, or proof cards can count as successful utilization

label: sk-design has stable workflowMode labels, but the deterministic benchmark scores intent/resource primitives rather than a hard hub-route primitive
recommendation: Add a hard hubRoute lane that compares private expected.workflowMode/routeOutcome/workflowModes against parsed route output before D1-intra resource scoring.

label: Lane C already has the right public/private and contamination-lint mechanism, but private expected gold lacks workflowMode and minimal-pair fields
recommendation: Extend private expected gold with workflowMode, workflowModes, routeOutcome, minimalPairGroup, pairRole, forbiddenWorkflowModes, and reasonTag; extend contamination lint to ban mode labels, packet names, aliases, resource basenames, and route-output tokens from public prompts.

label: Existing modePrecision is explicitly advisory and cannot enforce parent-hub workflowMode selection
enforcement: ENFORCEABLE for reporting the gap; ADVISORY in the current scorer
recommendation: Do not repurpose modePrecision; add a first-failing-stage hub-route gate before routed-intra/discovered.

label: sk-design route regressions are near-neighbor confusions, so the corpus needs adversarial minimal pairs rather than only one happy-path fixture per mode
enforcement: ENFORCEABLE on the corpus; ADVISORY for future language outside the corpus
recommendation: Author T1 alias coverage, T2 blind holdout prompts, and T3 adversarial minimal-pair groups for interface/foundations/motion/audit/md-generator plus bundle/defer cases.

label: For parent-mode skills, expected workflowMode or routeOutcome must pass before resource recall, context manifests, or proof cards can count as successful utilization

direction: Use existing modePrecision as the sk-design hub routing gate
reason: scoreModePrecision is explicitly advisory, never gated, and reports against expected.mode rather than a hard workflowMode corpus stage.
